title: fees::select_notes uses saturating_add in accumulation, masking overflow
description: fees.rs select_notes() accumulates note values with saturating_add (line ~140) while select_notes_spendable() in tx_builder.rs uses checked_add. If note values from a corrupt DB sum past u64::MAX, saturating_add silently clips to u64::MAX. The debug_assert at line ~157 then re-sums with plain .sum::<u64>() (also saturating) and confirms the invariant on the clamped value. The function returns Ok with an incorrect selection whose actual value is less than target+fee. Since select_notes operates on Note (not SpendableNote) and is called from fees.rs, the mismatch with select_notes_spendable creates two functions with inconsistent contracts. Fix: use checked_add in the accumulation loop to match select_notes_spendable, and return AmountOverflow on overflow.
